perturbation.original_text: Potential Purchaser shall maintain the confidentiality of those portions of the Evaluation Materials that contain confidential and/or proprietary information, and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose, thos e portions of the Evaluation Materials to any third party, except as otherwise set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Purchaser shall maintain the confidentiality of those portions of the Evaluation Materials that contain confidential and/or proprietary information. Potential Purchaser may, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose, those portions of the Evaluation Materials to any third party.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows Potential Purchaser to disclose confidential information to third parties without the Company's consent, directly contradicting the initial requirement for prior written consent. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 1, where the modified text permits disclosure that the original text strictly prohibits.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Potential Purchaser recognizes and acknowle dges that the Evaluation Materials may contain confidential and/or proprietary info rmation regarding the Company. Potential Purchaser shall maintain the confidentiality of those portions of the Evaluation Materials that contain confidential and/or proprietary information. Potential Purchaser may, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose, those portions of the Evaluation Materials to any third party, except as otherwise set forth herein. Potential Purchaser may disclose the Evaluation Materials to its parents, subsidiaries, parents’ subsidiaries, or affiliates (collectively, the “ Affiliated Entities ”) and Potential Purchaser’s and/or the Affiliated Entities’, directors, officers, employees, pa rtners, consultants, potential financing sources, joint-venture partners, bankers, accountants, lenders, investors, insurance consultants and/or brokers, attorneys, agents and financial and legal advisors (collectively, such entities or persons to whom Potential Purchaser or an Affiliated Entity discloses the Evaluation Materials, “ Representatives ”) who may need access to the Evaluation Materials, in Potential Purchase r’s discretion, for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser shall (i) inform in writing all of its Representatives who receive any portion of th e Evaluation Materials of the confidential and proprietary nature thereof and of th is Agreement and (ii) direct all such Representatives in writing to comply with the confidentiality and non-use terms of this Agreement. Potential Purchaser shall keep those portions of the Evaluation Materials confidential, as hereinabove provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows the Potential Purchaser to disclose confidential information to third parties without the Company's consent. This directly contradicts the initial obligation, found in both the original text and the contradicted text, that required prior written consent for such disclosure. The contradicted text states that 'Potential Purchaser shall maintain the confidentiality of those portions of the Evaluation Materials that contain confidential and/or proprietary information,' and the changed text removes the requirement for prior written consent before disclosing those materials. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making the agreement unenforceable and creating a strong in-text contradiction, fitting the definition of a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction, since the altered clause now has conflicting implications with the clause in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: Potential Purchaser shall not use any of th e Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than to evaluate the Proposed Transaction. The Company acknowledges and agrees that Potential Purchaser and its Affiliated Entities and Representatives may invest in or consider or manage investments in companies that compete either directly or indirectly with the Company and that this Agreement sh all in no way be construed to prohibit or restrict Potential Purchaser’s or its Affilia ted Entities or Representatives’ ability to make or manage or consider such investments.
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Purchaser shall not use any of th e Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than to evaluate the Proposed Transaction or to directly compete with the Company. The Company acknowledges and agrees that Potential Purchaser and its Affiliated Entities and Representatives may invest in or consider or manage investments in companies that compete either directly or indirectly with the Company and that this Agreement sh all in no way be construed to prohibit or restrict Potential Purchaser’s or its Affilia ted Entities or Representatives’ ability to make or manage or consider such investments.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text adds 'or to directly compete with the Company,' which allows Potential Purchaser to use the Evaluation Materials to directly compete. However, the second sentence maintains the original sentiment that nothing restricts Potential Purchaser's ability to invest in competing companies. This creates contradiction in Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Potential Purchaser shall not use any of th e Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than to evaluate the Proposed Transaction or to directly compete with the Company. The Company acknowledges and agrees that Potential Purchaser and its Affiliated Entities and Representatives may invest in or consider or manage investments in companies that compete either directly or indirectly with the Company and that this Agreement sh all in no way be construed to prohibit or restrict Potential Purchaser’s or its Affilia ted Entities or Representatives’ ability to make or manage or consider such investments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'or to directly compete with the Company,' allowing the Potential Purchaser to use Evaluation Materials for direct competition. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The second sentence in both texts states that the agreement does not restrict the Potential Purchaser's ability to invest in competing companies. The contradiction arises because the first part of the sentence allows direct competition using the evaluation materials, while the second part implies that the agreement does not restrict the potential purchaser's ability to compete, creating a conflict within the same sentence in Section 2.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not apply to, and Poten tial Purchaser shall have no obligation with respect to, any information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by Pote ntial Purchaser, or actual knowledge by Potential Purchaser of breach by its Representatives, (ii) was already known by Potential Purchaser or a third party, or in Potential Pu rchaser’s possession prior to the date of this Agreement, (iii) becomes available to Po tential Purchaser on a non-confidential basis from a source, other than the Company or its agents, which is or was not known to be prohibited from disclosing such portions of the Evaluation Materials by a contractual or legal obligation to the Company, (iv) is inde pendently developed or acquired by Potential Purchaser without violation of this Agreemen t, or (v) is or was of a non-proprietary nature.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall not apply to, and Poten tial Purchaser shall have no obligation with respect to, any information, regardless of how it becomes available to the public or was previously known by Potential Purchaser. The prior obligations are still in place. (i) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by Pote ntial Purchaser, or actual knowledge by Potential Purchaser of breach by its Representatives, (ii) was already known by Potential Purchaser or a third party, or in Potential Pu rchaser’s possession prior to the date of this Agreement, (iii) becomes available to Po tential Purchaser on a non-confidential basis from a source, other than the Company or its agents, which is or was not known to be prohibited from disclosing such portions of the Evaluation Materials by a contractual or legal obligation to the Company, (iv) is inde pendently developed or acquired by Potential Purchaser without violation of this Agreemen t, or (v) is or was of a non-proprietary nature.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'regardless of how it becomes available to the public or was previously known by Potential Purchaser' directly contradicts the subsequent specific exceptions (i)-(v). It creates uncertainty by suggesting that the agreement *never* applies to publicly available information, even if the information was leaked by Potential Purchaser, which conflicts with part (i) that explicitly states it only applies if the information was not leaked by the potential purchaser. This contradiction affects how section 3 would be interpreted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall not apply to, and Poten tial Purchaser shall have no obligation with respect to, any information, regardless of how it becomes available to the public or was previously known by Potential Purchaser. The prior obligations are still in place. (i) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by Pote ntial Purchaser, or actual knowledge by Potential Purchaser of breach by its Representatives, (ii) was already known by Potential Purchaser or a third party, or in Potential Pu rchaser’s possession prior to the date of this Agreement, (iii) becomes available to Po tential Purchaser on a non-confidential basis from a source, other than the Company or its agents, which is or was not known to be prohibited from disclosing such portions of the Evaluation Materials by a contractual or legal obligation to the Company, (iv) is inde pendently developed or acquired by Potential Purchaser without violation of this Agreemen t, or (v) is or was of a non-proprietary nature.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the phrase 'regardless of how it becomes available to the public or was previously known by Potential Purchaser,' which directly contradicts the subsequent specific exceptions (i)-(v) that outline situations where the agreement *does* apply. This contradicts the original intention as outlined in the Perturbation explanation by implying the agreement never applies to publicly available information, even if the Potential Purchaser caused the information to be available. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, meaning that the change causes an in-text contradiction. The original text specifies conditions under which the agreement *does* apply to information. The change makes it unclear when the specific conditions in (i)-(v) apply because of the contradicting introductory phrase. The phrase 'The prior obligations are still in place' is not enough to resolve the contradiction because it doesn't specify *which* prior obligations, especially given the preceding contradictory statement.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that Potential Purchaser, its Affiliated Entities, and/or its Representatives receive a request or are required to disclose any of the Evaluation Materials, pursuant to any applicable law, rule, regulation, regul atory authority, subpoena, order, summons, lawsuit, or other applicable judicial or gove rnmental order, or any other legal process, Potential Purchaser shall provide the Compa ny with prompt notice so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy at the Company’s expense.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that Potential Purchaser, its Affiliated Entities, and/or its Representatives receive a request or are required to disclose any of the Evaluation Materials, pursuant to any applicable law, rule, regulation, regul atory authority, subpoena, order, summons, lawsuit, or other applicable judicial or gove rnmental order, or any other legal process, Potential Purchaser is not required to provide the Compa ny with prompt notice to seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy at the Company’s expense.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says that a prompt notification to the Company should occur when a disclosure of any Evaluation Materials is being requested or required. However the changed text says that notification is not required. This in-text contradiction creates confusion on whether the company is required to know about disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that Potential Purchaser, its Affiliated Entities, and/or its Representatives receive a request or are required to disclose any of the Evaluation Materials, pursuant to any applicable law, rule, regulation, regul atory authority, subpoena, order, summons, lawsuit, or other applicable judicial or gove rnmental order, or any other legal process, Potential Purchaser is not required to provide the Compa ny with prompt notice to seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy at the Company’s expense. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not timely obtained, or the Company waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, Potential Purchaser, its Affiliated Entities, and/or its Representativ es may furnish and disclose any or all of the Evaluation Materials. Notwithstanding th e foregoing, notice to the Company shall not be required where disclosure is in connection with a routine audit or examination by, or a blanket document request from, a government al or regulatory authority having or claiming to have authority to regulate or oversee Potential Purchaser’s business, provided that Potential Purchaser informs such gove rnmental or regulatory authority of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates prompt notification to the Company regarding any request to disclose Evaluation Materials. The changed text explicitly states that such notification is 'not required'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which discusses the procedure if notification is not given, implying that notification is usually required, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction about the notification obligation.

perturbation.original_text: Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser further unde rstands and acknowledges that the Company does not make any representations or warrantie s as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials or any portion thereof. Potential Purchaser agrees that neither the Company nor its agents shall have any liab ility to Potential Purchaser or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of th e Evaluation Materials by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser further understands and acknowledges that the Company provides full representations and warranties as to the accuracy and completeness of the Evaluation Materials, ensuring their reliability. Potential Purchaser agrees that neither the Company nor its agents shall have any liab ility to Potential Purchaser or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of th e Evaluation Materials by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The text originally states that the 'Company does not make any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials'. The modified text states that the Company now provides 'full representations and warranties' regarding the material. This creates a contradiction in Section 7, where the Company at first disclaims responsibility, then takes it on.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser further understands and acknowledges that the Company provides full representations and warranties as to the accuracy and completeness of the Evaluation Materials, ensuring their reliability. Potential Purchaser agrees that neither the Company nor its agents shall have any liab ility to Potential Purchaser or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of th e Evaluation Materials by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Company does not make any representations or warranties. The changed text explicitly states that the Company 'provides full representations and warranties as to the accuracy and completeness of the Evaluation Materials'. This is a direct and irreconcilable contradiction. The Contradicted Text and Changed Text are at the same location, the Change creates a contradiction to the material regarding whether or not warranties are provided.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this agreement shall be the earlier of: (i) six (6) months from the date hereof; or (ii) the consummation of a Proposed Transac tion even if such Proposed Transaction is between the Company (or its Representatives) and a non-related third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this agreement shall be perpetual and ongoing indefinitely. The term of this agreement shall be the earlier of: (i) six (6) months from the date hereof; or (ii) the consummation of a Proposed Transac tion even if such Proposed Transaction is between the Company (or its Representatives) and a non-related third party.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text starts with stating that agreement shall be perpetual and ongoing indefinitely which is immediately followed by stating that the term of agreement can only be the earlier of two events. Therefore, this creates a In-Text contradiction as stated previously.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be executed in two or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same agreement, provided each Party receives a signed counterpart of the other Party. Facsimile signatures or otherwise electronically transmitted signatures (such as via e-mail in pdf format) may be used with the same fo rce and effect as original signatures. The term of this agreement shall be perpetual and ongoing indefinitely. The term of this agreement shall be the earlier of: (i) six (6) months from the date hereof; or (ii) the consummation of a Proposed Transac tion even if such Proposed Transaction is between the Company (or its Representatives) and a non-related third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text defines the agreement's term as either six months or the consummation of a transaction. The changed text adds a statement that the agreement is 'perpetual and ongoing indefinitely' *before* restating the original limited term. This directly conflicts with the subsequent clause stating the term is limited to the earlier of two events, thus creating an in-text contradiction. This is a structural flaw that places a clause in multiple locations with conflicting implications as stated in the definitions.
